column36: Comments

Found an interesting loop with forge, Hercules and Mr immortal. Dirty work lowers the attack of any villain I fight in the sewers by two, lowering Mr immortals attack to 0. The Hercules's Crowd favorite rescues a bystander every time I defeat a villain. Repeat until the bystander deck Is empty. Then get lucky with Son of Zeus for 60/60 and win the game in one turn. Very fun setup with recruit staying valuable through the game instead of trailing off when you don't want to recruit more heroes.

This was a unique challenge to be sure. It was cool to see an Epic villain for a change. Ancient One came in clutch. I thought about Angel a lot but opted for X-Force Wolverine to combo with Domino and basically make one deck a recruit and the other attack.

Originally thought Nightmare was a Nightmare to fight but game one showed the power of having a BFG on the side. Able to use that to get it out of the city or out of the Astral plane. The issue with game one however is the Angel/Hercules deck didn't get any Angel cards and ran out of recruit from all the KO's. Also having an escape slowed the game down a touch.

Game two however, first four draws was bystander into 3 scheme twists making the scheme a non-factor and Angel really pulled through. The angel deck only had 9 cards left and was able to constantly cycle and discard the Angel card for an extra bystander every turn when drawing. 

Also finding the strategy this time in game two, to navigate Nightmare with strategic escapes with their astral plane baddies to not harm the decks too badly as I got to choose which non-gray hero to lose. (Sidekicks helped in that regard.) while cleaning up the Astral Plane on the same turn with recruit to keep Nightmare out of there until it was time to take a tactic again.

My first attempt was rough, a victory with 3 escapes (2 from the astral plane) and 2.2 ppt. Goliath didn't do what I thought so I shifted to Angel, giving me more card draw. I also chose to focus on recruiting officers / heroes with recruit early on in my second attempt to manage the Astral Plane better. Imagine my surprise when BFG and Against All Odds were in the opening HQ. I did not mulligan, recruited both fairly early in the game, and had a much more successful game in my second attempt.

We got extremely lucky with the HQ and Villain Deck in the first game. We had both the Forge and Domino rares in the HQ early and Versatile meant that they were purchased quickly. Our Villain Deck gave us a lot of Henchmen early, which made keeping the City clean easier, and we didn't get any MS (2 were primed to come out in the next 3 turns if play had continued). We almost lost to the Scheme because we were prioritizing the MM, but that is a good problem to have.
Shouldn't have even gone for 2nd attempt.

We had lots of good options in the HQ without ever getting any rare cards.  The discard a card function powered P1 and tech machine P2.  We could not manage the Astral Plane well enough to prevent an escape.  This set up was terrific.  Lots of fun.
Leaving Squirrel Girl in the bank, killed my first game. Dr. Strange ritual artifacts helped alot in game 2.

First game was going fine. Then i thought "Eh, i can get a rare AND a mm hit, i can ignore the scheme for one turn, right? Oops." And literally the next turn i thought "Well, i can get 2 more mm hits, i wont draw the third twist in a row, right?" Wrong. In the second game i feasted the 2nd hand to 2 Hiroim ramps and rare every turn. I rescued Wolvie 3 times, stacked most twists on the bottom to be extra safe. The other hand got Skrull's rare with Spike's help, it even got a hit on mm too.
Hulking off the melk

This was a tough one! I didn't even win the first round and I went in worried, but Hiroim showed up and we were able to build big hands consistently before too long. Once the city was safely managed we could start clearing Goblin!

column36: Loooong game due to managing all those hidden witnesses! There were some nice 4 cost cards in pre-selected heroes so thought it might be good to go ALL IN Fantastic Four with the added choices! Invisible Woman and Fantastic Four United worked really well in this set up, helping to manage wounds and produce plenty of recruitment to use towards hidden witnesses or focus into attack with cards like human torch. Managed to also get Invisible Woman's legendary which helped curtail some of the ambush cards of Goblin Freak Show cards.

It was an interesting setup but felt like it dragged on quite a bit, especially in the second game in which I ran out of cards in the villain deck. I might give this one another go with different heroes some time, but honestly I was not a big fan of the hidden witnesses overall.
Hidden Witnesses are a pain, but made for a fun game trying to work around them. Player 2 got Hiroim's rare in both games, but we still couldn't avoid an escape.

I knew I was in trouble from the beginning because the three heroes in the setup are three of my least favorites (so I also have very little experience with them). I probably didn't pick very good heroes for the setup. But again, I just didn't know what would work well with those three. Curious to see what others do.
We decided to wipe our first game by turn 10 because we had drawn 6 heralds, two STs and a MS. Play 2 had a much smoother start. The only problem is that player 2's deck self-destructed too fast. They were getting 6-8 attack almost every turn, but not enough recruit to also handle the hidden witnesses. We also just weren't drawing heroes with recruit from the hero deck, so that made the game really slow. Player 1 eventually got together a good miek+daredevil deck. Miek's rare helped a lot with cleaning up the hidden witnesses.
The problem might have just come down to bad luck, but this was one of our least favorite games. Just poor synergy with the heroes/villains/scheme.

First game was fun with the FF4, but couldn't get through the witnesses and ran out of time. Got Joe's rare mid-game and coupled that with Hiroim's answer to the Black Widow and he went nuclear by the end of game. Great setup. Found it to be quite challenging. Lots of moving parts.

column36: That was an absolutely great and fun game. We loved the Celestial boon affects. It was really cool two bystanders every time we fought a villain. We thought it would be really hard to defeat the mastermind, but there were so many options to lower both the villains and the masterminds that it was definitely manageable the whole time. It was seriously one of the most fun games we’ve had in a while. Great job to the winner last season for this set up.

We went well in the first game but really struggled in the second. In the first we had a lucky villain deck draw, getting some good celestial boons out early. We felt this set up was a 3.5 for fun, as sometimes playing with lots of 2 costing cards isn't our favourite. We enjoyed the villains/mastermind/scheme combo though. That was lots of fun.

I knew with the preselected heroes that I had to use I should go with the strategy of one hand going with the superior deck build while the other hand recruits all the cards that won't fit into that deck build. And this worked amazingly! Not only did the Spider's do an amazing job taking out the mastermind, they also made quick work of the celestials, which was super helpful because of how powerful those boons were in this setup.

column36: Spiders! P1 ended up being all 2 cost Spiders and P2 was mostly Cuckoos with a few Spider-Woman cards. Both decks were lacking on recruit by the end but double Nezarr and 3 early Save the Days on the Spider side ramped to be enough to not even need shatter by the end. I did have a funny moment when a Stepford Cuckoo ascended to be a MM when a tactic left them alone in the city and a Twist came the next turn.

Very fun set up! Getting the Exitar/Sentinel loop going is a low but efficient way to thin the decks espeically with Gammenon to net you an extra bystander everytime. Tiamut plus Spiders is jsut asking for trouble too
I mean, it was fun, enthrone is always fun and swinging at big MM is fun. Not having turns that generate attack and recruit...not fun....
Really struggled with this one. Managed to get both Nezarr, The Calculators on one hand, so I basically just fought Exodus with that.
Definitely one of the hardest setups I’ve ever played and very challenging. If not for Shadow Spider and Radioactive Spider, to be my main cards.

You know, our first game went VERY bad. To the point where we did not want to play a second game. But decided last minute before deadline to play a second one. It was not as bad as the first one. It definitely still took way longer than I'd liked. In the second game (the one submitted), one deck struggled a lot more than the other and developed late due to the HQ not really being in favor, finding shatter recruit points where you can due to lower attacking characters was key here.

column36: Slow scheme paired with a deck-building puzzle of a mastermind, and if you give me an opportunity to stack SHIELD heroes, I'm going to do it.  MCU side concentrated on Deathlok, Comic side grabbed Mockingbird and Coulson, and Kitty Pride was used to cycle Groot cards out of the HQ.  Hit the 8th Twist but thanks to a Ultron-Pym-discounted Infinity Formula I was able to stop the evilwincon.  Went hard on Undercover to take some of the pain off of the Master Strikes and at the end, even Coulson's Orbital Strikes was able to "honorably" take on Morgan with a single card.

I chose to lean into the undercover mechanic which allowed one hand to send all of their starting cards undercover.  Then I manipulated the HQ so AKP's disrupt circuits would hit the mastermind each time.  This was my favorite setup so far.

column36: Strategy ended up being let my better half focus on building up one hero (in this case Agent Caulson) to take down Morgan Fay. Be end of the game, didn't have much to do on my turns as city was easy to clear and couldn't attack the mastermind. Kind of a alog of a game, but still fun for being different. Would have likes more time to try again, but didn't have it this week. Would probably have gone with another shield agent.

My first attempt was disastrous. I was in good shape in game 2 until Ultron Prime wiped out a rare I had been about to buy. Had to shift gears from Deathlok to Mockingbird and luckily pulled out a last turn Final Blow. Whew! I HATE Chivalrous Duel with a passion.

column36:
That was a very tough one (but also one of the most interesting setup), with multiple threats looking out for the player. 

Coulson did well with mockingbird, I could make a red Coulson+Bobby deck, which could start hitting Epic Morgan, and if I would have had 1 or 3 more turns I could have won. The 9 attack, CD is very tough, but the SHIELD rares both could get trough it. 

But, damn, giving out wounds and giving back 0 cost card is also evil. For until around turn 20-25 I could only manage to keep our decksize at the same as in the beginning, and clearing up the constant in-flow of wounds. 

But in the end I disliked it mainly because of the scheme, and its HQ dependency. If I would get the ApocKitty tech, 2 recruit card early, I would at least had the chance to buy some ant-man cards. But without any tech cards in a pool and drawing the high-cost ant-man cards early. This 
essentially became a strong (8 out of 11) timer with a mastermind where a quick victory is hard to achievet (both because of the CD and the dual threat of the city and wounds). 

OldMan Logan turned out to be an irrelecant pick here, the second hand went for Groot, and could sort of manage to heal all the wounds we got. In advance I would have  taken Drax, but he was not available. Now I would take maybe Jocasta or Deathlok more probably, to provide some help with the scheme and also some shield . 

But neither have time (or the strong urge) to play this again; even if it got exciting aroun turn 30, before ending due to scheme timer.

column36: This was a tough one! Trying to balance the recruit to buy the shrink tech and get Chivalrous Dual attack proved tricky. I went back and forth on heroes and would’ve tried at least 1 different with a second attempt. Namor didn’t pay off the way I’d hoped, but didn’t see Imperious Rex. Coulson, however, was my best hero. I was able to recruit Fake But Inspiring Death and build to SHIELD level 8 rather quickly, so he was able to get 3 hits off on Le Fey. But the scheme got the best of me.
Fun set up. Went for black widow synergy. It didn’t work well the first game but second game was much better.

Bit of a divided opinion on this setup for us. I played a Yelena/Doom 2099 deck that absolutely sang. She had a Ravage/Surfer deck that kept getting bad draws and poor twist timing. Since I'm inputting the score, I erred on the higher side :) This was the first time I'd played this scheme, and it really short circuits the planning you can do.
Fun setup!
First game, Human Torch was KO’d by turn 4. Leaned into Red Guardian + Yolena and have found a new favorite hero combo.

Good choice with villain groups, they really caused some trouble when combined with Sinister in reducing hand size. Dark Memories and Fated Future really made a difference, as did getting enough recruit so I could get good things to replace the shield troopers lost to Phalanx. I got to hit Sinister a couple of times by recruiting a dual card that boosted Dark Memories, so that was satisfying.

Trying to manage the rightmost HQ space to avoid the Heroes being "Lost in Grief" is actually quite doable.  Though I'm not sure it makes enough of a difference to prioritize recruiting sub-optimal heroes.

A tough scheme but it made for a thrilling victory after losing first attempt.  I believed in the hero choices and stuck with them.  2nd game went much better, and player 1 was able to pick up 2 copies of Ravage Detect Vibrations, which became incredibly helpful and probably key to victory.  Being able to stave off and prepare for Twists was massive.  Spider-Man 2099 helped player 1 with card draw and getting just enough attack to beat Sinister.  Player 2 grabbed Yelena and Red Guardian (including Guardian's rare fairly early) along with Nightcrawler to compliment and support with just enough power again, and also grabbed the third Detect Vibrations later in the game which did not hurt at all.  Red Guardian's Ruse uncommon came in clutch for both players in the final turns.

column36: Thought the villain and scheme set up was very fun and had put good pressure on us as players, but I thought the hero selection was a little rough, and we weren’t able to come up with something that clicked the way we hoped.

I think I've been getting pretty lucky so far this tournament. Game went well. Some strong heroes. Haven't gotten to use 2099 much since I got it and it has a lot of fun and powerful heroes. Hulk 2099 was kind of a waste of a pick, but I suppose that's natural with this scheme. He can combo w/ Torch, it just didn't line up and feels a bit awkward to get going to me. My second game I'm in the middle of and it isn't going nearly as well, just playing worse. Fun scheme though.

Interesting setup to try to find good hero choices and decisions to manage the scheme. I thought Jean Grey would be a slam dunk choice because of the bystander-focused setup, but I ended up rescuing ALL of the bystanders in my 1st attempt with her! It made Mr. Sinister and villains easier to fight as there were no more bystanders for them to kidnap, but it also completely nerfed Jean's rare and the bystander rescue engine I had built. I didn't realize how small the bystander deck would be with the small starting size and 12 dedicated to the Scheme setup. New achievement unlocked though! I finally closed out my 1st attempt with an infinite Yelena. Aggressive KOing with Invisible Woman and a lot of Nightcrawler teleports let me setup my deck to go infinite with Yelena, despite having only 1 of each of the cards needed. Two poorly timed Ra'ktar villains meant unfortunate escapes (3 total) which led me to submit my 2nd attempt instead, despite higher VP (102) and VPpt (3.09) in 1st. Will be curious to see how that result would have ranked. Hard to choose. 2nd attempt I used Red Guardian. Was really fun and strong synergy with Yelena. Used Invisible Woman in both attempts for her red cards and aggressive KO potential to streamline my decks and try to build towards Yelena combo and consistent red cards. Red Guardian's 4-cost common was also great with Invisible Woman's Four of a Kind common. I always underrate that card for IW, but it's such a strong rate and not too hard to enable. I'm curious to try the setup again with Red Guardian and Spider-man 2099 because of their potential Undercover and Cyber-Mod synergy. Was interesting to manage the scheme. I recruited from the HQ to try to leave the heroes that I didn't want in the rightmost space, so the scheme would KO them and get me closer to recruiting key cards for the other heroes. (Sorry Human Torch and Ravage!)

I had two games that were too great, so I struggled to choose my pick. I'll go with this one because... I made an strategy involving Magneto work (And 1 less turn for the win, too)! By filling myself with Bindings, I could theoretically use them to protect my great cards against the unpredictability of the setup. And it worked! It was both Green Goblin and Mags' work that took care of the big hits, and the other side did great, too! Although they drowned in clutter by the end of the game, which I expected to happen. My second attempt was amazing too. No other things to say: This games were just chef's kiss.~

Ended up winning game 1 but got super unlucky with early twists pretty much KOing whatever we happened to recruit which then lead to an escape. Game 2 was much better. Really liked the preset hero choices and available hero choices. We had not played with Mystique or Falcon/WS in a while but I think they meshed perfectly with Darkhawk, Green Goblin, and Moon Knight. Loved the setup!

Decided I needed to play this one REAL sneaky, only recruiting the bare minimum of non-grey Heroes, making good use of Undercover, and using lots of grey Sidekicks and Shield Officers. Worked ok in the first game and P2 got a solid Green Goblin deck going that did most of the work for a decent score. The HQ was much kinder on the second attempt and gave me lots of Red Guardian commons. Early on I unleashed two copies of his Attack common for the first hit, and got the ‘repeat this’ Feast tactic to thin out several starters. That deck then pretty much solo’d the game.

This game made you think about how you played cards and what you bought. Needed to make sure I had lots of sidekicks and shield agents to get eaten with all the feasting that was happening. I think that the scheme would be fun and the mastermind would be fun on separate occasions but together they are not a pleasant combination.
Even with the loss, very fun and challenging.

While we appreciate the difficulty of what the scheme and Mastermind were doing, it was frustating to keep losing our good cards and have that contribute to the Evil Wins condition. Sabretooth was great at keeping the Feast KOs from getting the good cards, but we couldn't do anything about the Twists and Master Strikes. We lost both games, but came closest in the first match.
I went hard. I went fast. I crashed. I burned. I'm happy :)

A lot of luck in this setup because of the frequent re-shuffling of the villain deck. I liked the setup though with heroes that don't get to see the table very often so it caused us to think through lots of creative options. Our first game the city was overwhelming throughout, but the second game we got luckier with a decent pacing of cards so we were able to stay on top of it.

In our first game after seeing the HQ cycling and how many heroes ended up in the KO pile at the end we took a chance that we could see Stingray's Rare card which beefs up with the more heroes in the KO pile. It came up about halfway through the game but took a while to gather the recruit necessary. Only got to use it 1 time as "the finisher" for 14 strength on the last term.

Curious if others have the same Bastion strategy as we did - we prioritized striking the main card all 5 times first before worrying about the subsequent masterminds. This was so that we got the KOs and potential good bystander effects as early as possible (and even the fifth time without any Fight benefits but at least stopping new MMs from spawning) -- it meant that we had to face a few master-strikes with 4 or 5 consequences, though.

column36: This one was a lot of fun. Venom rocket and shatterstar did well in this run, me using the former alongside beta ray bill, and my wife the latter with Solo. We got all of the master strikes which drew it out, but we had a ton of fun with this round. Cloning, KO'ing heroes, and a ton of masterminds, what's not to love?

column36:
I really enjoy the wounding mechanic from the Black Panther expansion. Such a fun way to whittle down our enemies. It wasn't the first time playing anything from the villain deck. 
It was my first time using Super-Skrull. His mechanics were alright. I didn't find myself with excess attack very often so he wasn't as effective as I'd hoped. What ended up happening was both decks were built similarly due to the HQ filling out. I wanted a deck of all fours and one with all attack cards. The left ended up with a bit of both, resulting in huge recruiting turns and the Warriors Three rare is off the chain. It amassed 10 attack nearly every turn. Right side just kind of was there. Cleaning the city for the most part and did a good job protecting from the Scheme. 
Fun setup though! It was also my first time with the FF United. I understand their gimmick, but it fell flat. Warriors Three for me was the star.
Slow start but both decks were very fun.

Funny story, totally forgot to shuffle the bystanders in on our first game, but it ended up going better than the other playthrough. We were able to stay on top of the city and were able to rescue the vibranium pretty without much trouble. In hindsight, maybe we should've let 1 or 2 some of it escape and put more wounds on Killmonger.

it went alright, afraid to end up low in pod for the 2 escapes again XD

Fun setup.  First play thru was horrible, couldn't control the vibranium as it got lucky attaching to high attack heroes and hands never came together (can't count the amount of 4 recruit 2 attack turns drawn, was ridiculous).  Attempt two had much better draws.  Though the vibranium attunement stole two my heroes rares to the bottom of the deck!!!  I had forgotten how much of a rhythm you need to take out Killmonger.  She-Hulk was a definite great choice.  Skaar helped a lot in my second attempt.  Valkyrie cards did not hurt, but didn't make either hand shine.  F4 United are always a win.  Joe Fixit was ok, but not a lot of 6+ villains to transform his card.  Could have chosen better on him, but I wanted more recruit when I chose him and he did provide that.

One of my decks stayed a little too lean and didn't have enough buying power to buy the desired cards in HQ until right at the end, which slowed me down. Galactus Hungers was summoned by midgame by the other deck, but I only got to use him twice. Held back on having him smash spaces because I had several Bridge Conqueror cards without enough cards to let destroyed spaces count.

I probably should have prioritized the Mastermind more but I was really tryiing to keep my escaped villain count down compared to the previous attempt, so I kept having to prioritize attacking the villains to accomplish that. And even then I still had a bad few turns where three escaped in a row.
None

"RUN....while you still have the chance."
-Chuck Norris.
As always, thank you for all that goes into putting this on!

Fun setup, lots of synergy with the heroes.  I used the suggested heroes because I'm terrible at figuring out which heroes work best with each other and the villain setup, so I love having some suggestions.  The second game we got really lucky and most of the masterstrikes were in the bottom of the deck, so we didn't draw them and the twists were spread out enough that we could keep the bystanders from being captured.  Lots of fun, would play again!
